Macros


#define RAIN_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS 100.0

Functions

Function Documentation

EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE void mirror_cleanup (void )

Definition at line 101 of file mirror.c.

101                           {
102   if (g_digital_rain) {
103     digital_rain_destroy(g_digital_rain);
104     g_digital_rain = NULL;
105   }
106   if (g_last_rain_output) {
107     SAFE_FREE(g_last_rain_output);
108     g_last_rain_output = NULL;
109   }
110   options_state_destroy();
111   platform_destroy();
112 }




References digital_rain_destroy(),

EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE
  char * mirror_convert_frame (uint8_t * rgba_data, int src_width, int
  src_height)


Definition at line 119 of file mirror.c.


119                                                                               {
120   if (!rgba_data || src_width <= 0 || src_height <= 0) {
121     return NULL;
122   }
123 
124   g_frame_count++;
125   // Derive a time value from frame count using actual target FPS from options
126   int target_fps = (int)GET_OPTION(fps);
127   if (target_fps <= 0) {
128     target_fps = 60; // Default to 60 FPS if not set
129   }
130   float time_seconds = (float)g_frame_count / (float)target_fps;
131 
132   // Get current settings from options or terminal detection
133   int dst_width = (int)terminal_get_effective_width();
134   int dst_height = (int)terminal_get_effective_height();
135   color_filter_t filter = (color_filter_t)GET_OPTION(color_filter);
136   terminal_color_mode_t color_mode = (terminal_color_mode_t)GET_OPTION(color_mode);
137   if (g_frame_count <= 3 || g_frame_count % 120 == 0) {
138     log_debug("frame=%u color_filter=%d color_mode=%d", g_frame_count, (int)filter, (int)color_mode);
139   }
140   if (color_mode == TERM_COLOR_AUTO) {
141     color_mode = TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR; // xterm.js supports truecolor
142   }
143   palette_type_t palette_type = (palette_type_t)GET_OPTION(palette_type);
144   bool aspect_ratio = true; // Preserve webcam aspect ratio
145   bool stretch = false;     // Don't stretch - maintain proportions
146 
147   // Build terminal capabilities structure with user's color mode
148   terminal_capabilities_t caps = {0}; // Zero-initialize all fields first
149   caps.color_level = color_mode;
150   caps.capabilities = 0;
151   caps.color_count = (color_mode == TERM_COLOR_NONE)  ? 0
152                      : (color_mode == TERM_COLOR_16)  ? 16
153                      : (color_mode == TERM_COLOR_256) ? 256
154                                                       : 16777216;
155   caps.utf8_support = true;
156   caps.detection_reliable = true;
157   caps.render_mode = (render_mode_t)GET_OPTION(render_mode);
158   caps.wants_background = false;
159   caps.wants_padding = true; // Enable padding for centering in mirror mode
160   caps.palette_type = palette_type;
161   caps.desired_fps = 60;
162   caps.color_filter = filter;
163 
164   // Convert RGBA to RGB (strip alpha channel) and optionally flip horizontally
165   rgb_pixel_t *rgb_pixels = SAFE_MALLOC(src_width * src_height * sizeof(rgb_pixel_t), rgb_pixel_t *);
166   if (!rgb_pixels) {
167     return NULL;
168   }
169 
170   bool flip = GET_OPTION(flip_x);
171   for (int y = 0; y < src_height; y++) {
172     for (int x = 0; x < src_width; x++) {
173       int src_x = flip ? (src_width - 1 - x) : x;
174       int src_idx = (y * src_width + src_x) * 4;
175       int dst_idx = y * src_width + x;
176 
177       rgb_pixels[dst_idx].r = rgba_data[src_idx + 0];
178       rgb_pixels[dst_idx].g = rgba_data[src_idx + 1];
179       rgb_pixels[dst_idx].b = rgba_data[src_idx + 2];
180       // Alpha (rgba_data[src_idx + 3]) is discarded
181     }
182   }
183 
184   // Apply color filter to pixels if needed (except rainbow - handled in ANSI stage)
185   if (filter != COLOR_FILTER_NONE && filter != COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW) {
186     // color_filter operates on packed RGB24 format
187     uint8_t *rgb24 = (uint8_t *)rgb_pixels;
188     int stride = src_width * 3;
189     apply_color_filter(rgb24, src_width, src_height, stride, filter, time_seconds);
190   }
191 
192   // Create image structure
193   image_t img = {.w = src_width, .h = src_height, .pixels = rgb_pixels, .alloc_method = IMAGE_ALLOC_SIMD};
194 
195   // Get palette characters based on selected palette type
196   const char *palette_chars;
197   switch (palette_type) {
198   case PALETTE_BLOCKS:
199     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_BLOCKS;
200     break;
201   case PALETTE_DIGITAL:
202     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_DIGITAL;
203     break;
204   case PALETTE_MINIMAL:
205     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_MINIMAL;
206     break;
207   case PALETTE_COOL:
208     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_COOL;
209     break;
210   case PALETTE_CUSTOM:
211     // Custom palette uses user-provided characters from options
212     palette_chars = GET_OPTION(palette_custom);
213     if (!palette_chars || palette_chars[0] == '\0') {
214       palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_STANDARD; // Fallback
215     }
216     break;
217   case PALETTE_STANDARD:
218   default:
219     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_STANDARD;
220     break;
221   }
222 
223   // Convert to ASCII using capability-aware function
224   char *ascii_output =
225       ascii_convert_with_capabilities(&img, dst_width, dst_height, &caps, aspect_ratio, stretch, palette_chars);
226 
227   // Clean up
228   SAFE_FREE(rgb_pixels);
229 
230   if (!ascii_output) {
231     log_error("ascii_convert_with_capabilities returned NULL");
232     return NULL;
233   }
234 
235   // Apply rainbow color filter to ANSI output by replacing RGB values
236   // This preserves character selection while applying rainbow colors
237   if (filter == COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW) {
238     char *rainbow_output = rainbow_replace_ansi_colors(ascii_output, time_seconds);
239     if (rainbow_output) {
240       SAFE_FREE(ascii_output);
241       ascii_output = rainbow_output;
242     }
243   }
244 
245   bool matrix_rain = GET_OPTION(matrix_rain);
246   if (matrix_rain) {
247     // Initialize digital rain context if needed or dimensions changed
248     if (!g_digital_rain || g_digital_rain->num_columns != dst_width || g_digital_rain->num_rows != dst_height) {
249       if (g_digital_rain) {
250         digital_rain_destroy(g_digital_rain);
251       }
252       if (g_last_rain_output) {
253         SAFE_FREE(g_last_rain_output);
254         g_last_rain_output = NULL;
255       }
256       g_digital_rain = digital_rain_init(dst_width, dst_height);
257       if (!g_digital_rain) {
258         log_error("Failed to initialize digital rain effect");
259         return ascii_output; // Return without effect on error
260       }
261       g_last_rain_update_time = emscripten_get_now();
262     }
263 
264     // Update color from active filter (allows filter changes after initialization)
265     digital_rain_set_color_from_filter(g_digital_rain, filter);
266 
267     // Time-based updates: only update effect every RAIN_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS
268     double current_time = emscripten_get_now();
269     double elapsed_ms = current_time - g_last_rain_update_time;
270 
271     if (elapsed_ms >= RAIN_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS) {
272       // Calculate actual delta time in seconds
273       float delta_time = (float)(elapsed_ms / 1000.0);
274       g_last_rain_update_time = current_time;
275 
276       // Apply effect and cache a copy
277       char *rain_output = digital_rain_apply(g_digital_rain, ascii_output, delta_time);
278       if (rain_output) {
279         // Free old cached output
280         if (g_last_rain_output) {
281           SAFE_FREE(g_last_rain_output);
282         }
283         // Cache a copy (we'll return the original rain_output)
284         g_last_rain_output = strdup(rain_output);
285         // Return the rain output (caller will free it)
286         SAFE_FREE(ascii_output);
287         ascii_output = rain_output;
288       }
289     } else if (g_last_rain_output) {
290       // Use cached output
291       SAFE_FREE(ascii_output);
292       ascii_output = strdup(g_last_rain_output);
293     }
294   } else {
295     // Clean up digital rain context if it exists but effect is disabled
296     if (g_digital_rain) {
297       digital_rain_destroy(g_digital_rain);
298       g_digital_rain = NULL;
299     }
300     if (g_last_rain_output) {
301       SAFE_FREE(g_last_rain_output);
302       g_last_rain_output = NULL;
303     }
304     g_last_rain_update_time = 0.0;
305   }
306 
307   return ascii_output;
308 }






References apply_color_filter(),
    ascii_convert_with_capabilities(), aspect_ratio(),
    rgb_pixel_t::b, terminal_capabilities_t::capabilities,
    terminal_capabilities_t::color_count,
    terminal_capabilities_t::color_filter, COLOR_FILTER_NONE,
    COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW, terminal_capabilities_t::color_level,
    terminal_capabilities_t::desired_fps,
    terminal_capabilities_t::detection_reliable,
    digital_rain_apply(), digital_rain_destroy(),
    digital_rain_init(), digital_rain_set_color_from_filter(),
    rgb_pixel_t::g, GET_OPTION, IMAGE_ALLOC_SIMD,
    log_debug, log_error, PALETTE_BLOCKS,
    PALETTE_CHARS_BLOCKS, PALETTE_CHARS_COOL,
    PALETTE_CHARS_DIGITAL, PALETTE_CHARS_MINIMAL,
    PALETTE_CHARS_STANDARD, PALETTE_COOL, PALETTE_CUSTOM,
    PALETTE_DIGITAL, PALETTE_MINIMAL, PALETTE_STANDARD,
    terminal_capabilities_t::palette_type, rgb_pixel_t::r,
    RAIN_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS, rainbow_replace_ansi_colors(),
    terminal_capabilities_t::render_mode, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, TERM_COLOR_16, TERM_COLOR_256,
    TERM_COLOR_AUTO, TERM_COLOR_NONE, TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR,
    terminal_get_effective_height(),
    terminal_get_effective_width(),
    terminal_capabilities_t::utf8_support,
    terminal_capabilities_t::wants_background, and
    terminal_capabilities_t::wants_padding.






EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE
  void mirror_free_string (char * ptr)


Definition at line 311 of file mirror.c.


311                                    {
312   SAFE_FREE(ptr);
313 }






References SAFE_FREE.






EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE
  int mirror_init_with_args (const char * args_json)


Initialize mirror mode with command-line style arguments


Parameters


args_json JSON array of argument strings, e.g.
  ["mirror", "--width", "80",
  "--height", "40"]


Returns


0 on success, -1 on error


Definition at line 47 of file mirror.c.


47                                                  {
48   WASM_LOG("mirror_init_with_args: START");
49   WASM_LOG("mirror_init_with_args: After START log");
50 
51   // Initialize platform layer
52   WASM_LOG("Calling platform_init...");
53   WASM_LOG("mirror_init_with_args: About to call platform_init");
54   asciichat_error_t err = platform_init();
55   WASM_LOG("mirror_init_with_args: platform_init returned");
56   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
57     WASM_ERROR("platform_init FAILED");
58     return -1;
59   }
60   WASM_LOG("platform_init OK");
61 
62   // Initialize logging to stderr (console.error in browser)
63   WASM_LOG("Calling log_init...");
64   log_init(NULL, LOG_DEBUG, true, false);
65   // Use debug-style format in WASM so browser console shows full log headers
66   log_set_format(OPT_LOG_TEMPLATE_DEFAULT_DEBUG, false);
67   WASM_LOG("log_init OK");
68 
69   // Parse space-separated arguments
70   WASM_LOG("Parsing arguments...");
71   char *args_copy = NULL;
72   char *argv[64] = {NULL};
73   int argc = wasm_parse_args(args_json, argv, 64, &args_copy);
74   if (argc < 0) {
75     WASM_ERROR("strdup FAILED");
76     return -1;
77   }
78   WASM_LOG_INT("Parsed arguments, argc", argc);
79 
80   // Initialize options (sets up RCU, defaults, etc.)
81   WASM_LOG("Calling options_init...");
82   err = options_init(argc, argv);
83   free(args_copy);
84 
85   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
86     WASM_LOG_INT("options_init FAILED", err);
87     return -1;
88   }
89   WASM_LOG("options_init OK");
90 
91   // Initialize ANSI color code generation (dec3 cache for RGB values)
92   WASM_LOG("Calling ansi_fast_init...");
93   ansi_fast_init();
94   WASM_LOG("ansi_fast_init OK");
95 
96   WASM_LOG("mirror_init_with_args: COMPLETE");
97   return 0;
98 }






References ansi_fast_init(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    LOG_DEBUG, log_init(), log_set_format(),
    OPT_LOG_TEMPLATE_DEFAULT_DEBUG, options_init(),
    platform_init(), WASM_ERROR, WASM_LOG,
    WASM_LOG_INT, and wasm_parse_args().
